_comment: Month 1 guided recipes (Menu A + B), authored verbatim from docs/T2D_Beginner_Edition.md. Self-contained Coach content with steps inline — NOT in the read-only recipes.json. Doneness verdicts use the cited USDA rules in doneness.json (e.g. whole cuts stay 145°F even where the plan suggests med-rare).

recipes.title: Sausage & Spinach Egg Bites
recipes.note: Makes 12. Fridge 4 days; freeze the rest. Reheat 30–45 sec. Eat 3 per person.

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Grease all 12 muffin cups well, or use silicone liners — egg bites cement themselves to bare metal.
In a skillet over medium-high, cook 8 oz sausage, breaking it up, until no pink remains (~6 min). Drain and let cool a few minutes.
steps.sensory_cue: Deep-browned crumbles, glistening rendered fat, no raw red.

steps.text: Whisk 10 eggs with ¼ cup cream and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Stir in the cooked sausage, 5 oz chopped spinach, and 1 cup shredded cheddar. Pour evenly into the cups, about ¾ full.
Bake 18–20 min until the centers are set with no liquid jiggle. Cool 5 min, then lift out.
steps.sensory_cue: Tops puffed and matte and springy; the center has at most a faint wobble (firm jello, not liquid).

steps.text: Cook the chicken first (great for Sunday): preheat the oven to 400°F. Rub a chicken breast with oil, salt, and pepper. Roast on a sheet pan 22–25 min until 165°F, then rest 5 min and slice.
steps.sensory_cue: White all the way through with clear juices.

steps.text: Pour 2 tbsp dressing into the bottom of each jar.
Add 4 oz sliced chicken, 1 chopped hard-boiled egg, 1 crumbled bacon strip, 2 tbsp blue cheese, and ¼ cup halved tomatoes.
Pack romaine on top to keep everything dry. Seal and refrigerate up to 4 days.
Morning-of: add ½ diced avocado. At lunch, shake to coat.
Preheat the oven to 425°F.
Cut 1.5 lb broccoli into florets. Toss with 2 tbsp olive oil, ½ tsp garlic powder, and salt. Spread on a sheet pan in one layer.
Rub chicken thighs with oil and salt and nestle them among the broccoli.
Roast 20–25 min until the broccoli edges brown and the chicken reads 165°F.
steps.sensory_cue: Broccoli edges blistered; chicken white throughout with clear juices.

recipes.title: Beef & Pepper Stir-Fry
recipes.note: Thin stir-fry strips cook too fast to thermometer reliably — go by browning. If you check the pan beef, 145°F is the safe whole-cut minimum.

steps.text: Slice 1 lb steak thinly against the grain and pat dry. Slice 2 bell peppers and ½ onion.
steps.sensory_cue: Cut across the meat's fibers, not along them — that's what makes a slice tender instead of chewy.

steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a skillet over high until it shimmers.
Add the beef in one layer; don't stir for 1 min, then sear 2–3 min until browned. Remove.
steps.sensory_cue: Edges turn from red to deep brown; it releases from the pan when it's ready.

steps.text: Add the peppers and onion; stir-fry 4–5 min until crisp-tender.
Return the beef; add 2 tbsp soy sauce and 1 minced garlic clove; toss 1 min.
Thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp: soak frozen shrimp in cold water 15 min, then drain and pat very dry. Make zoodles from 3 zucchini with a spiralizer or peeler ribbons.
steps.sensory_cue: Dry shrimp sears; wet shrimp steams and turns rubbery — pat them really dry.

steps.text: Melt 3 tbsp butter in a skillet over medium. Add 3 minced garlic cloves and cook 30 sec — don't let them brown.
Add the shrimp in one layer; cook about 2 min per side until pink and curled into a loose C. Remove.
steps.sensory_cue: Pink/coral and opaque, curled into a loose C. A tight O means overcooked — the window is about 30 seconds, so hover.

steps.text: Add the zoodles; toss 2 min just to warm — any longer makes them watery.
Return the shrimp; finish with a squeeze of lemon and parsley.
Make cauliflower mash from 1 head: boil florets ~10 min until fork-tender, drain, and press out the water before blending with butter, cream, and salt.
steps.sensory_cue: Press in the colander until cloudy water stops streaming and the surface looks dry — skipping the drain makes soup.

steps.text: Pat 2 pork chops dry and season both sides.
Heat 1 tbsp oil over medium-high. Sear the chops about 4–5 min per side until 145°F.
steps.sensory_cue: Deep golden crust; the center reaches 145°F.

steps.text: Rest 3 min, then serve over the mash.
Form 1 lb ground beef into 2 patties and season.
Heat a skillet over medium-high. Sear the patties 3–4 min per side until 160°F.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink in the center; juices run clear.

steps.text: Make a bed of chopped romaine in 2 bowls.
Top each with a patty, ¼ cup cheddar (it melts on the hot patty), tomatoes, and sugar-free ketchup/mustard.

recipes.title: Vanilla Chia Pudding + Berries
recipes.note: No cooking. Savory swap: ham & cheese egg muffins using the egg-bite method.

steps.text: In a bowl, whisk 6 tbsp chia seeds, 1.5 cups almond milk, ½ tsp vanilla, and sweetener to taste.
Wait 5 min, then whisk again to break up any clumps.
Divide into 5 jars and chill overnight — it sets to a pudding.
Each morning, top one jar with ¼ cup berries.
Sunday: thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp, then sauté in 1 tbsp oil over medium-high about 2 min per side until pink and curled. Chill.
steps.sensory_cue: Pink, opaque, curled into a loose C.

steps.text: Build each box: 5 oz shrimp, romaine, ¼ cup tomatoes, 2 tbsp dressing on the side, and a lime wedge.
Add ½ avocado morning-of.
Season 1 lb chicken with fajita seasoning; sear over medium-high 5–6 min per side until 165°F, then slice. (Skip if your chicken is pre-cooked.)
steps.sensory_cue: White throughout with clear juices.

steps.text: Slice 2 peppers and ½ onion; sauté in 1 tbsp oil over medium-high 6 min until soft and charred at the edges.
In another pan, sauté 2 bags cauliflower rice in 1 tbsp oil 5 min.
Build bowls: cauliflower rice, chicken, peppers and onion, topped with cheese.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Mix 1.25 lb ground meat with 1 egg, ¼ cup mozzarella, and salt/pepper. Roll about 16 balls.
steps.sensory_cue: The mix should be tacky and hold together when pinched — too wet? add a spoon of almond flour or parmesan.

steps.text: Bake on a sheet pan 18 min until 160°F.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink at the center of a split meatball.

steps.text: Warm marinara in a pot; add the meatballs and simmer 5 min.
Make zoodles from 3 zucchini; toss in a hot dry pan 2 min. Serve the sauce and meatballs over the zoodles.
Thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp. Trim 1.5 lb green beans.
Sauté the green beans in 3 tbsp butter over medium-high 6 min until crisp-tender.
Push the beans aside; add the shrimp and 3 minced garlic cloves; cook about 2 min per side until pink and curled.
steps.sensory_cue: Loose C, pink and opaque throughout.

recipes.title: Steak & Roasted Asparagus
recipes.note: The plan suggests medium-rare (130–135°F); 145°F is the USDA safe minimum the coach checks against.

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Toss 1.5 lb asparagus with oil and salt; roast 12 min.
Pat 1 lb steak dry and season. Heat a skillet over high; sear 3–4 min per side.
steps.sensory_cue: Deep mahogany crust. For safety, cook to 145°F; a 3-minute rest follows.

steps.text: Rest 5 min, then slice against the grain.
steps.sensory_cue: Slice perpendicular to the visible fibers so each piece is tender, not stringy.

steps.text: Slice 14 oz smoked sausage into coins. Brown in a skillet over medium-high 5 min (it's already cooked — you're just browning).
Add ½ shredded cabbage, ½ sliced onion, and 2 tbsp butter.
Sauté 10 min until the cabbage is tender.

recipes.title: Western Omelet Egg Muffins
recipes.note: Makes 12. Cool 5 min and lift out. Eat 3 per person.

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Grease 12 muffin cups or use liners.
Sauté ½ diced onion and 1 diced pepper in a little oil over medium-high 4 min until soft. Cool a few minutes.
Whisk 10 eggs with ¼ cup cream and salt and pepper. Stir in the veg, 6 oz diced ham, and 1 cup cheddar.
Fill the cups ¾ full. Bake 18–20 min until set (~160°F). Cool 5 min, then lift out. Eat 3 per person.
steps.sensory_cue: Tops puffed and matte and springy; the centers set with at most a faint wobble.

steps.text: Pour 2 tbsp ranch into the bottom of each jar.
Add 1.5 oz ham, 1.5 oz turkey, 1 chopped hard-boiled egg, 1 oz cheddar, and ¼ cup tomatoes.
Pack romaine on top to keep everything dry. Seal and refrigerate.
Shake at lunch to coat.

recipes.title: Salisbury Steak + Mushroom Gravy & Green Beans

steps.text: Form 1 lb ground beef into 2 oval patties; season.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a skillet over medium-high. Sear the patties 3–4 min per side until 160°F. Remove.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink in the center; juices run clear.

steps.text: In the same pan, sauté 8 oz sliced mushrooms 5 min until browned.
Add ¾ cup beef broth and scrape up the brown bits (deglaze). To thicken, whisk in ⅛ tsp xanthan gum (or simmer 8 min to reduce). Simmer 2 min.
Return the patties to warm. Steam 1 lb green beans 5 min alongside.

recipes.title: Creamy Tuscan Chicken over Cauliflower Mash

steps.text: Make cauliflower mash from 1 head — boil florets until fork-tender, drain well, and press out the water before blending with butter, cream, and salt.
steps.sensory_cue: Press in the colander until the water stops streaming — draining well is what keeps it from going soupy.

steps.text: Season 1.5 lb chicken; sear over medium-high 5–6 min per side until 165°F. Remove and slice.
steps.sensory_cue: White throughout with clear juices.

steps.text: In the same pan, add ½ cup cream, ¼ cup chopped sun-dried tomatoes, and 3 oz spinach; simmer 3 min until the spinach wilts.
Return the chicken; serve over the mash.
Thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp. Slice 12 oz sausage.
steps.sensory_cue: Dry shrimp sears; wet shrimp steams and turns rubbery — pat them really dry.

steps.text: Brown the sausage with 1 sliced pepper over medium-high 6 min.
Add the shrimp and 1 tbsp Cajun seasoning; cook 2–3 min per side until pink and curled.
steps.sensory_cue: Pink and opaque, curled into a loose C; a tight O means overcooked.

steps.text: Pat 2 chops dry; season. Sear over medium-high 4–5 min per side until 145°F. Remove and rest.
steps.sensory_cue: Deep golden crust; the center reaches 145°F.

steps.text: Sauté 8 oz mushrooms and 1 garlic clove 5 min. Add ¼ cup cream; simmer 2 min (a pinch of xanthan gum to thicken).
Return the chops to coat.
Slice 1 pepper and ½ onion; sauté in 1 tbsp oil over medium-high 6 min. Remove.
Add 1 lb shaved steak to the hot pan; cook 3–4 min, breaking it apart, until no longer pink.
steps.sensory_cue: Edges turn from red to deep brown; thin shaved steak cooks too fast to thermometer — go by color.

steps.text: Return the veg, lay 4 oz provolone on top, and cover 1 min to melt.
Preheat the oven to 375°F. Grease 12 muffin cups.
Whisk 10 eggs with ¼ cup cream and salt. Fold in 1.5 cups finely chopped broccoli and 1 cup cheddar.
Fill the cups ¾ full. Bake 20 min until set (~160°F). Eat 3 per person.
steps.sensory_cue: Tops puffed and matte and springy; the centers set with at most a faint wobble.

steps.text: Roll 3 oz turkey around 1 oz cheddar.
Pack with ½ sliced cucumber, ¼ cup peppers, and 5 olives, with 2 tbsp ranch on the side.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Slice 1.5 lb chicken into thin cutlets; pat dry.
Beat 1 egg in one bowl; put ½ cup almond flour in another. Dredge: dip chicken in egg, then almond flour.
Heat 2 tbsp oil over medium-high; fry the cutlets 3 min per side until golden.
steps.sensory_cue: Golden, crisp crust on both sides.

steps.text: Top each with marinara and mozzarella; bake 8 min until the chicken is 165°F and the cheese melts. Serve with a side salad.
steps.sensory_cue: Cheese melted and bubbling; chicken white throughout at 165°F.

steps.text: Brown 1 lb ground beef over medium-high 6 min until 160°F; drain.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink crumbles remain; juices run clear.

steps.text: Stir in taco seasoning and 2 tbsp water; simmer 2 min.
Serve over chopped romaine; top with ½ cup cheddar and tomatoes.
Thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp; toss with blackening seasoning.
steps.sensory_cue: Dry shrimp sears; wet shrimp steams and turns rubbery — pat them really dry.

steps.text: Slice 2 zucchini; sauté in butter over medium-high 4 min. Remove.
Add the shrimp; cook 2–3 min per side until pink and curled. Combine.
steps.sensory_cue: Pink and opaque, curled into a loose C.

steps.text: Brown 1 lb ground pork over medium-high 6 min until 160°F.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink crumbles remain; juices run clear.

steps.text: Add ½ head shredded cabbage, 2 tbsp soy, 1 tsp sesame oil, and grated garlic and ginger.
Stir-fry 8 min until the cabbage is tender.
Slice 1 lb sausage links, 2 peppers, and ½ onion.
Brown the sausage over medium-high 6 min.
Add the peppers, onion, and garlic; sauté 10 min until soft (the sausage should reach 160°F).
steps.sensory_cue: Peppers soft and the sausage cooked through to 160°F with no pink.

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Grease all 12 muffin cups well, or use silicone liners — egg bites cement themselves to bare metal.
Whisk 10 eggs with ¼ cup cream and a pinch of salt. Fold in 3 oz chopped spinach and ¾ cup feta.
Fill the cups ¾ full. Bake 18 min until set (~160°F).
steps.sensory_cue: Tops puffed and matte and springy; the center has at most a faint wobble, not liquid.

steps.text: Pack each box: 4 oz cooked chicken, romaine, ½ cucumber, ¼ cup tomatoes, 2 tbsp feta, and 5 olives.
Keep 2 tbsp oil + vinegar on the side; dress at lunch.
If using wooden skewers, soak them in water 30 min so they don't char and snap.
Cube 1.5 lb chicken; toss with oil, lemon, oregano, and salt. Thread onto skewers.
Grill or broil 4–5 min per side until 165°F.
steps.sensory_cue: White all the way through with clear juices.

steps.text: Salad: slice 2 cucumbers and ½ red onion; toss with dill, oil, vinegar, and salt.
Make tzatziki: 1 cup Greek yogurt + ½ cucumber grated and squeezed dry + 1 grated garlic clove + 1 tbsp lemon + chopped dill + salt. Stir.
Mix 1 lb ground beef with cumin, coriander, chopped parsley, and salt. Form into 6 logs.
Broil or pan-sear 10–12 min, turning, until 160°F. Serve with tzatziki and a cucumber-tomato salad.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink at the center; juices run clear.

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp — wet shrimp steams, dry shrimp sears.
steps.sensory_cue: Pat them really dry with paper towels before they go in.

steps.text: In an oven-safe skillet, simmer 1 cup crushed tomatoes with 1 minced garlic clove 5 min.
Nestle in the shrimp; scatter ½ cup feta on top.
Bake 10 min until the shrimp are pink and opaque. Serve with a green salad.
steps.sensory_cue: Pink/coral and opaque, curled into a loose C.

steps.text: Cube 1 lb pork; toss with lemon, oregano, oil, and salt.
Sear over medium-high 6–8 min until 145°F.
steps.sensory_cue: Deep golden crust; the center reaches 145°F.

steps.text: Build bowls over cucumber, tomato, olives, and feta.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Slice 1 lb sausage, 2 peppers, and ½ onion.
Toss with oil on a sheet pan in one layer.
Roast 25 min, stirring once, until the sausage is 160°F and the veg are soft.
steps.sensory_cue: Sausage browned with no pink inside; pepper edges blistered and soft.

recipes.title: Greek Yogurt & Walnut Parfait
recipes.note: No cooking. Add the berries morning-of so they stay fresh.

steps.text: Layer per jar: ¾ cup full-fat Greek yogurt + 2 tbsp walnuts + 1 tsp flax.
Each morning, add ¼ cup berries to one jar.
Pack each box: 4 oz cooked chicken, 2 oz fresh mozzarella, ¼ cup tomatoes, and basil.
Keep 1 tbsp balsamic + oil on the side; dress at lunch.
Season 1.5 lb chicken; sear over medium-high 5–6 min per side until 165°F. Slice; toss with ¼ cup pesto.
steps.sensory_cue: White throughout with clear juices.

steps.text: Make ribbons from 2 zucchini with a peeler. Sauté 2 min; top with parmesan.
Preheat the oven to 375°F. Brown 1 lb ground beef 6 min; stir in ½ cup marinara and a pinch of cinnamon.
steps.sensory_cue: Browned crumbles with no raw red remaining.

steps.text: Slice 2 zucchini lengthwise. Layer zucchini and beef sauce in a baking dish; top with ½ cup mozzarella.
Bake 25 min until bubbly and golden.

recipes.title: Lemon Garlic Shrimp over Cauliflower Rice

steps.text: Sauté 2 bags cauliflower rice in 1 tbsp oil 5 min; set aside.
Thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp. Melt butter, add garlic 30 sec, add shrimp 2 min per side until pink. Finish with lemon and parsley; serve over the rice.
steps.sensory_cue: Pink/coral and opaque, curled into a loose C.

recipes.title: Pork Tenderloin + Roasted Green Beans & Tomatoes

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Pat 1 lb tenderloin dry; season; rub with oil.
Sear all sides in an oven-safe skillet 4 min, then roast 15–18 min until 145°F. Rest 5 min; slice.
steps.sensory_cue: Deep golden crust; the center reaches 145°F before the 5-minute rest.

steps.text: Roast 1 lb green beans and 1 cup tomatoes on a sheet pan 18 min alongside.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Mix 1 lb ground turkey with 1 egg, parmesan, and salt. Roll ~16 balls.
Bake 18 min until 165°F.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink at the center of a split meatball.

steps.text: Simmer in ½ cup marinara 5 min. Wilt 5 oz spinach in a pan 1–2 min; serve under the meatballs.

recipes.title: Chorizo & Egg Breakfast Bowls
recipes.note: Makes 5 containers; reheat 45 sec each morning.

steps.text: Render 8 oz chorizo + 1 diced pepper in a skillet over medium-high 6 min until cooked through (160°F).
steps.sensory_cue: Deep-browned crumbles, glistening rendered fat, no raw red remaining.

steps.text: Pour in 8 beaten eggs; scramble gently 2–3 min until just set.
steps.sensory_cue: Soft, glossy curds that are just set, not wet or runny.

steps.text: Stir in ¼ cup cheddar. Portion into 5 containers; reheat 45 sec each morning.

recipes.title: Beef Taco Salad Jar
recipes.note: Serve cold. Add the avocado morning-of so it stays fresh.

steps.text: Sunday: brown 1 lb beef with taco seasoning until 160°F; cool.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink in the crumbles; juices run clear.

steps.text: Build each jar: 2 tbsp salsa on the bottom, then 3 oz beef and 1 oz cheddar, then romaine on top.
Add ½ avocado morning-of.
Marinate 1.5 lb chicken in tikka marinade (½ cup Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp garam masala, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp paprika, grated garlic & ginger, 1 tbsp lemon, salt) at least 30 min — ideally Sunday.
Broil or sear over medium-high 5–6 min per side until 165°F.
steps.sensory_cue: Charred edges; white all the way through with clear juices.

steps.text: Sauté 1 bag cauliflower rice 5 min; serve the chicken over it.

recipes.title: Korean-Style Beef Bowls
recipes.note: Thin sliced beef cooks too fast to thermometer reliably — go by browning.

steps.text: Slice 1 lb steak thin against the grain.
steps.sensory_cue: Cut perpendicular to the visible fibers so each slice is tender, not chewy.

steps.text: Mix sauce: 2 tbsp soy, 1 tsp sesame oil, grated garlic + ginger, sweetener.
Sear beef in a hot skillet 3 min; add the sauce, toss 1 min. Serve over sautéed cauliflower rice.
steps.sensory_cue: Edges turn from red to deep brown; the beef releases from the pan when it's ready.

steps.text: Thaw & dry 1 lb shrimp: soak frozen shrimp in cold water 15 min, then drain and pat very dry.
steps.sensory_cue: Dry shrimp sears; wet shrimp steams and turns rubbery — pat them really dry.

steps.text: Simmer 1 cup coconut milk + 1 tbsp curry powder in a skillet 3 min.
Add shrimp; simmer 4–5 min until pink and opaque. Serve over cauliflower rice.
steps.sensory_cue: Bright pink and opaque, curled into a loose C. A tight O means overcooked — the window is about 30 seconds.

steps.text: Cut 1.5 lb pork shoulder into chunks; season with cumin, lime, garlic, salt.
Pressure-cook 45 min, or slow-cook 6–8 hr, until it shreds easily. Shred with two forks.
steps.sensory_cue: The pork pulls apart with light pressure from two forks.

steps.text: Optional: crisp the shreds in a hot skillet 3 min. Spoon into butter lettuce cups with cilantro.
Brown 1 lb ground turkey over medium-high 6 min until 165°F.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink in the crumbles; juices run clear.

steps.text: Off the heat, stir in lime juice, 1 tbsp soy, chili flakes, chopped onion + cilantro.
Spoon into butter lettuce cups.

recipes.title: Chocolate-PB Chia Pudding Cups
recipes.note: No cooking. Chill overnight in 5 jars.

steps.text: Per cup, whisk 1.5 tbsp chia + ⅓ cup almond milk + 1 tsp cocoa + 1 tsp peanut butter + sweetener.
Chill overnight in 5 jars — it sets to a pudding.
Toss 4 oz shredded cooked chicken with 1 tbsp buffalo sauce.
Pack over romaine + celery with 1 tbsp blue cheese; ranch on the side.
Cube 1 lb chicken; sear in a hot skillet 5 min until 165°F. Remove.
steps.sensory_cue: White throughout with clear juices.

steps.text: Sauté 12 oz broccoli 5 min. Return chicken; add soy/sesame/garlic sauce + ¼ cup cashews; toss 1 min.

recipes.title: Mongolian-Style Beef + Broccoli
recipes.note: Thin sliced beef cooks too fast to thermometer reliably — go by browning.

steps.text: Slice 1 lb steak thin against the grain; sear in a hot skillet 3 min. Remove.
steps.sensory_cue: Cut across the fibers; edges sear from red to deep brown and release from the pan.

steps.text: Sauté 12 oz broccoli 5 min. Return beef; add soy/ginger/garlic/sweetener sauce; simmer 2 min to coat.
Thaw & dry 1 lb shrimp; toss with fajita seasoning.
steps.sensory_cue: Pat the shrimp very dry so they sear instead of steam.

steps.text: Sauté 2 peppers + ½ onion 6 min.
Add shrimp 2–3 min per side until pink. Serve over greens or cauliflower rice.
steps.sensory_cue: Bright pink and opaque, curled into a loose C. Pull them the second they curl.

steps.text: Brown 1 lb ground pork over medium-high 6 min until 160°F.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink in the crumbles; juices run clear.

steps.text: Off the heat, add lime, 1 tbsp soy, chili, garlic, cilantro. Spoon into butter lettuce.
Preheat oven 375°F. Halve 3 peppers; remove the seeds.
Brown 1 lb sausage 6 min; stir in ½ cup marinara.
steps.sensory_cue: Deep-browned crumbles, no raw red remaining.

steps.text: Fill the pepper halves; top with ½ cup mozzarella. Bake 25 min until peppers soften and cheese melts.
steps.sensory_cue: Peppers slumped and fork-tender; cheese melted and bubbling.
